jobs.title: Assistant Manager, Direct Sales - GM Energy
jobs.description: View All Jobs For Employers Join Log In   View All Jobs     Summary Generated by Built In Description About GM Energy GM Energy is transforming the energy and power industry with innovative solutions that integrate EV technology with home energy management. Our mission is to provide sustainable, decentralized energy solutions that reduce reliance on traditional grids and enhance energy resilience for customers. As a rapidly growing business unit within General Motors, we are looking for dynamic leaders to help drive our vision forward. The Role The Team Lead, Direct Sales is a critical leadership role within GM Energy, reporting directly to the Group Manager, Direct Sales. This role is responsible for overseeing a team of Home Energy Consultants (HECs), ensuring they meet performance targets, adhere to sales best practices, and deliver an outstanding customer experience. The Team Lead will focus on performance management, sales effectiveness, customer satisfaction, and operational efficiency, ensuring the success of their assigned team within the broader Direct Sales organization. What You'll Do (Responsibilities) Sales Team Leadership & Performance Management Lead, coach, and mentor a team of Home Energy Consultants (HECs) to achieve sales targets and conversion rates. Monitor individual and team performance metrics, including sales quotas, appointment conversion rates, and revenue targets. Conduct regular one-on-one coaching sessions and team meetings to provide guidance, performance feedback, and professional development opportunities. Ensure team members are executing GM Energy's sales methodologies and utilizing the sales playbook effectively. Oversee the adoption of sales tools and CRM systems to maintain accurate tracking of customer interactions, appointments, and sales data. Sales Process & Strategy Execution Maintain and refine the GM Energy sales playbooks, ensuring best practices, objection handling strategies, and sales scripts are consistently applied.

jobs.title: Key Account Director
jobs.description: View All Jobs For Employers Join Log In   View All Jobs     Summary Generated by Built In Who Are We? Postman is the world’s leading API platform, used by more than 45 million+ developers and 500,000 organizations, including 98% of the Fortune 500. Postman is helping developers and professionals across the globe build the API-first world by simplifying each step of the API lifecycle and streamlining collaboration—enabling users to create better APIs, faster. The company is headquartered in San Francisco and has offices in Boston, New York, Austin, Tokyo, London, and Bangalore - where Postman was founded. Postman is privately held, with funding from Battery Ventures, BOND, Coatue, CRV, Insight Partners, and Nexus Venture Partners. Learn more at postman.com or connect with Postman on X via @getpostman. P.S: We highly recommend reading The "API-First World" graphic novel to understand the bigger picture and our vision at Postman. The Opportunity We are looking for a Key Account Director to lead growth within a set of Postman’s most important enterprise accounts. jobs.title: Data Analyst
skills: Review, clean, and maintain large volumes of transactional and investment data.
Identify discrepancies, missing data, and inconsistencies requiring correction.
Prepare and support proof of claims related to securities litigation and class action settlements.
Manage and review custodian confirmations to validate securities claims.
Analyse investment transaction data and loss calculations using Excel, Access, and GPS tools
Compile, sort, organise, and present analytical results according to defined requirements.
Integrate data outputs from multiple systems and maintain them in secure repositories.
Export, update, and reconcile datasets on an ongoing basis as required
Accurately transfer data from PDF documents to Excel for routine and ad-hoc analysis.
Generate data summaries, overviews, and analysis reports upon request.
Efficiently transfer and input data across multiple applications and platforms.
Communicate findings, data issues, and analytical scenarios clearly to internal stakeholders.
Perform quality assurance (QA) checks and troubleshoot data-related issues.
Support additional tasks and responsibilities assigned by management.
Working knowledge of SQL, statistics, and data analysis methodologies
Advanced Microsoft Excel proficiency is mandatory. (Candidates without advanced Excel skills should not apply)
Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office , particularly Excel and Access
Fundamental understanding of financial concepts , including financial statements and derivatives
Experience or familiarity with Python, R, SPSS, or Bloomberg is an advantage.
Prior experience in data research, validation, and reporting is preferred.
Strong interest in data integration, analysis, and validation
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and research skills
Ability to work independently as well as collaboratively within a team
Proven ability to manage deadlines in high-pressure environments
Strong attention to detail with a data quality and QA mindset

This role is ideal for professionals with strong advanced Excel skills , a solid understanding of financial data, and hands-on experience working with large transactional datasets. The successful candidate will support data review, validation, and analysis functions critical to securities litigation and class action settlement processes. If you are passionate about data accuracy , enjoy solving complex data problems, and are comfortable working in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment, this Data Analyst job in Mumbai offers an excellent opportunity for career growth and exposure to real-world financial data operations. About the Role As a Data Analyst at DRRT , you will be responsible for reviewing, maintaining, and analysing investment transaction and loss calculation data. The role involves extensive use of Microsoft Excel (advanced level), Microsoft Access, DRRT GPS systems , and other analytical tools to ensure data accuracy and completeness. You will work closely with internal teams to identify data deficiencies, recommend corrections, and support the preparation of proof of claims for class action settlements .
